etymology.description: 士 (upside-down ritual axe) + 呂 (two blocks of metal) + phonetic 亼 (open mouth upside down) -> original pronunciation; no earth pictogram in ancient forms -> metal, later gold
etymology.interpretation: Initially representing copper, due to its use in the Copper Age, the character 金 (jīn, "metal") evolved to refer broadly to metal as technological progress made various metals crucial in society. The extension to gold reflects its value and prominence. The phonetic 亼 indicates the character's pronunciation, with semantic elements showing early associations with precious materials.

examples.chinese: 金是一种重要的工业原料(金是一種重要的工業原料)。
examples.pinyin: Jīn shì yī zhǒng zhòngyào de gōngyè yuánliào.
examples.english: Metal is an important industrial material.

examples.chinese: 她的项链是纯金的(她的項鍊是純金的)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de xiàngliàn shì chún jīn de.
examples.english: Her necklace is made of pure gold.

examples.chinese: 每个国家都有自己的金矿(每個國家都有自己的金礦)。
examples.pinyin: Měi gè guójiā dōu yǒu zìjǐ de jīnkuàng.
examples.english: Every country has its own gold mines.
